refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a KPI requirement that must be operationalized with measurable acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ CT_target), explicit duty cycle definition, and telemetry-based verification. The refined FR clarifies that cycle time is a system-level performance outcome dependent on vehicle dynamics, operator control responsiveness (including tele-operation latency), and safety constraints that may impose timing overhead.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Correlate with telemetry data (vehicle speed, acceleration, operator input latency, safety system activation events) to isolate performance bottlenecks.
sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_timing_overhead budget.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-2.1: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/electric actuation response time and travel speed within safety limits; allocate cycle time budgets to load, travel, and dump sub-cycles; implement deterministic control loops.

DP-2.2: Design teleoperation network architecture with bounded latency (e.g., dedicated low-latency link, edge compute, predictive command buffering) to keep operator-to-vehicle command latency ≤ latency_budget.

DP-2.3: Implement safety services as non-blocking, interrupt-driven handlers with worst-case execution time ≤ safety_timing_overhead; use safety-critical scheduling to prevent safety checks from blocking cycle progression.
DP-2.4: Instrument vehicle and teleoperation systems with cycle time telemetry collection (event timestamps, sub-cycle markers, latency probes) and expose via Telemetry interface for KPI reporting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrated approach: (1) Vehicle DP: Optimize actuation response and allocate deterministic cycle time budgets to load/travel/dump sub-cycles. (2) Teleoperation DP: Implement bounded-latency command delivery via dedicated network link and edge compute. (3) Safety Services DP: Enforce safety constraints with non-blocking, time-budgeted handlers. (4) Telemetry DP: Instrument all sub-systems to expose cycle time metrics (timestamps, latencies, safety event durations) via Telemetry interface.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time budget is coupled to teleoperation latency budget: if teleoperation latency exceeds budget, vehicle must wait, extending cycle time. This coupling is unavoidable but must be explicitly managed via latency_budget allocation.

Safety services timing overhead couples to vehicle actuation: safety checks that block or delay actuation directly impact cycle time. Non-blocking safety design mitigates but does not eliminate coupling.
Telemetry collection overhead: instrumentation for cycle time measurement may introduce jitter or latency if not carefully isolated from real-time control loops.
coupling_check.notes: The couplings are inherent to the problem domain (safety and teleoperation are real constraints on cycle time). Mitigation requires explicit latency budgets, non-blocking safety design, and careful telemetry instrumentation. Recommend decomposing into sub-FRs to isolate and manage each coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target and latency_budget are defined and achievable given vehicle hardware and network infrastructure.
Assumption: Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill) is stable and representative.
Risk: If teleoperation latency exceeds budget due to network congestion or edge compute overload, cycle time KPI cannot be met without vehicle redesign.
Risk: Safety system activation (e.g., emergency stop, collision avoidance) may impose unpredictable timing overhead, causing p95 cycle time to exceed target.
Risk: Telemetry collection overhead or data transmission delays may mask true cycle time performance.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to isolate vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry concerns. For each sub-FR, define explicit timing budgets (latency_budget, safety_timing_overhead, telemetry_overhead) and allocate to DPs. Establish a cycle time budget breakdown: CT_target = vehicle_time + teleoperation_latency + safety_overhead + measurement_margin.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration of vehicle, teleoperation, and safety services to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exposes cycle time metrics (load/travel/dump sub-cycle durations, operator latency, safety event durations, p95 aggregates) to Site Manager and System Engineer for KPI verification and performance analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, target cycle time) from Site Manager to configure vehicle and teleoperation parameters.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports safety-related timing events (safety system activations, latency violations, constraint breaches) to Safety Manager for risk assessment.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution coordinates teleoperation network latency management via Network Link to ensure operator command delivery meets latency_budget.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integrator and owns cycle time KPI. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution scope. Cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting are system-level concerns. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross boundary.

Vehicle owns actuation response time and sub-cycle execution (load/travel/dump). However, vehicle cycle time is coupled to teleoperation latency and safety constraints, which are external to Vehicle module. Vehicle must expose cycle time telemetry (sub-cycle timestamps) to NGHE Solution for aggregation. Diffusion: Vehicle depends on Telemetry interface and must coordinate with Teleoperation and Safety Services via NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation owns operator command latency and network link management. Teleoperation must deliver commands within latency_budget to preserve vehicle cycle time. Teleoperation exposes latency metrics via Telemetry interface. Diffusion: Teleoperation depends on Network Link interface and must coordinate with Vehicle via NGHE Solution.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement and timing overhead budgeting. Safety Services must implement non-blocking handlers to avoid blocking vehicle actuation. Safety Services exposes safety event durations and constraint violations via SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces. Diffusion: Safety Services must coordinate with Vehicle and Teleoperation via NGHE Solution.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because cycle time KPI is a system-level performance outcome that depends on coordinated behavior of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. NGHE Solution owns the cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) own specific timing concerns but must be coordinated at the system level.
containment_risks: Risk: If cycle time budget allocation is not explicit and enforced, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services may independently optimize for their own performance, leading to system-level cycle time overage.

Risk: Telemetry aggregation and KPI reporting require synchronized timestamps across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Clock skew or asynchronous telemetry collection may introduce measurement error.

Risk: Cycle time KPI is sensitive to duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain). If duty cycle is not stable or representative, measured p95 cycle time may not reflect real-world performance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit cycle time budget breakdown at NGHE Solution level: CT_target = vehicle_time_budget + teleoperation_latency_budget + safety_overhead_budget + measurement_margin. Allocate budgets to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. Implement centralized telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution with synchronized timestamps. Define duty cycle specification as input to Tasking interface.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-002) is decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) that address vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry concerns separately. The selected DP is an integrated approach that maps to all four sub-FRs, but the coupling between DPs (vehicle cycle time coupled to teleoperation latency and safety overhead) is not fully resolved at this level. Recommend proceeding to next decomposition level to isolate and manage couplings via explicit timing budgets.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface diffusion: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services must all expose cycle time metrics via Telemetry interface. Ensure telemetry schema is consistent and timestamps are synchronized.
Network Link interface diffusion: Teleoperation depends on Network Link to manage latency. Ensure Network Link interface provides latency visibility and control mechanisms.

SafetyAlert interface diffusion: Safety Services must report safety-related timing events via SafetyAlert. Ensure SafetyAlert schema includes timing information (event duration, constraint violation magnitude).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for system-level cycle time KPI. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that own specific timing concerns but must be coordinated at NGHE Solution level. Cycle time budget allocation and telemetry aggregation are system-level responsibilities that belong in NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Cycle time KPI can be verified by measuring p95 cycle time across ≥100 representative load→travel→dump cycles under agreed duty cycle conditions. Telemetry interface provides cycle time metrics (sub-cycle timestamps, latencies, safety event durations) for analysis. Verification is feasible if duty cycle is stable and telemetry collection is synchronized.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Cycle time KPI is allocated to NGHE Solution, which owns system-level integration and KPI reporting. Sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and telemetry sub-systems respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Decompose CR-NGHE-002 into sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) and proceed to next decomposition level to isolate and manage couplings.
Define explicit cycle time budget breakdown: CT_target = vehicle_time_budget + teleoperation_latency_budget + safety_overhead_budget + measurement_margin.
Establish duty cycle specification as input to Tasking interface (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill level).
Define telemetry schema for cycle time metrics (sub-cycle timestamps, latencies, safety event durations) to ensure consistency across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
Implement centralized telemetry aggregation in NGHE Solution with synchronized timestamps and p95 calculation.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI requirement that depends on coordinated behavior of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The refined FR is decomposed into four sub-FRs (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) that address specific timing concerns. The selected DP is an integrated approach that maps to all sub-FRs, but couplings between DPs (vehicle cycle time coupled to teleoperation latency and safety overhead) require explicit timing budget allocation and management at the next decomposition level. Recommend proceeding to decompose each sub-FR (FR-2.1 through FR-2.4) to isolate and manage couplings.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-2.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within allocated budgets.
FR-2.2: The teleoperation system shall deliver operator commands with latency ≤ latency_budget to preserve cycle time performance.
FR-2.3: The safety services shall enforce safety constraints without exceeding safety_timing_overhead budget.
FR-2.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report cycle time telemetry (start/end timestamps, sub-cycle durations, latency events) for KPI verification.

assumptions: CT_target and latency_budget are defined and achievable given vehicle hardware and network infrastructure.
Duty cycle definition (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill) is stable and representative.
Telemetry collection can be synchronized across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with acceptable clock skew.
Safety system activation (e.g., emergency stop, collision avoidance) is infrequent and does not dominate p95 cycle time distribution.
open_risks: If teleoperation latency exceeds budget due to network congestion or edge compute overload, cycle time KPI cannot be met without vehicle redesign.
Safety system activation may impose unpredictable timing overhead, causing p95 cycle time to exceed target.
Telemetry collection overhead or data transmission delays may mask true cycle time performance.
Duty cycle definition may not be stable or representative, leading to measured p95 cycle time that does not reflect real-world performance.
evidence_needed: Cycle time budget breakdown: CT_target = vehicle_time_budget + teleoperation_latency_budget + safety_overhead_budget + measurement_margin.
Duty cycle specification (load mass, travel distance, terrain, operator skill level).
Telemetry schema for cycle time metrics (sub-cycle timestamps, latencies, safety event durations).
Network latency characterization (mean, p95, p99) under agreed duty cycle conditions.
Safety system timing overhead characterization (activation frequency, duration, impact on cycle time distribution).
Measurement methodology for p95 cycle time (sample size, aggregation period, outlier handling).
